description: Forbidden temporal anti-patterns for static MDX articles. Static articles cannot revisit, update, or report back after publication — phrases that promise future updates or use relative time references decay on every reader visit.

patterns.description: Unfulfillable editorial promise — static MDX cannot revisit after publication. Rewrite as a tracker link or past-tense observation.
Unfulfillable editorial promise — static MDX cannot report back after publication. Rewrite as a tracker link or remove the forward commitment.
Implies future update that static MDX cannot deliver. Replace with a direct link to the resource the reader should consult.
Implies future broadcast — static MDX will not be updated. Remove or rewrite as a tracker/archive link.
Implies future content — static MDX does not self-update. Remove or rewrite with an absolute timeline if one is known.
Group-buy status phrase becomes stale after the window closes. Use absolute dates: 'the buy opened on YYYY-MM-DD and runs through YYYY-MM-DD'.
Imprecise year claim — use exact dates when the schedule is known. 'approximately [year]' misleads readers once the delivery schedule firms up.
Imprecise quarter claim — use an exact date or a firm quarter anchor without 'approximately'.
Imprecise month-name date claim — use exact dates when the schedule is firm.
Relative time reference decays after publication. Replace with an absolute date derived from the article's publishedAt.
Relative time reference decays after publication. Replace with an absolute date derived from the article's publishedAt.
Relative tracker reference decays after the week of publication. Replace with the ISO week anchor: 'the W19 tracker' or 'the 2026-W19 snapshot'.

Present-tense 'live Trends Tracker' implies real-time data that static MDX cannot provide — the link target is a snapshot from publication time. Replace with the specific ISO week anchor: 'the W19 Trends Tracker' and link to '/trends/tracker/<isoWeek>'.

Forward-looking 'tracker will' promise is unfulfillable — static MDX cannot speak for the tracker's future updates. Replace with 'Follow the [trends tracker](/trends/tracker) to see…' or remove the sentence.

Markdown-link form of the tracker-will anti-pattern — '[Trends Tracker](url) will ...' is an unfulfillable forward-looking promise. Rewrite as 'Follow the [Trends Tracker](url) to see ...' or remove the sentence.
